heroDesc: From DFM and tool trial to molded plastic part delivery
bodyTitle: Molding Service from plastic design to stable production
bodyDesc: Molding service is suitable for plastic parts that need repeatable production. The review focuses on shrinkage, wall thickness, draft, parting line, gate and cooling design. The process moves from DFM to tool design, trial correction and production delivery.

panels.title: Design Review
panels.subtitle: Wall / Draft
panels.body: Reduces sink, warp, trapped gas and assembly interference risk.

panels.title: Trial Loop
panels.subtitle: Sample Confirmation
panels.body: Trial samples verify dimensions, appearance and assembly status.

panels.title: Production Control
panels.subtitle: Stable Parameters
panels.body: Focus on molding parameters, material batches, first article and patrol checks.

tableRows: Wall, radius, undercut, gate, parting line
Design changes should be completed before tooling
Tool design, machining, assembly and trial
Trial count and correction scope depend on project
Resin grade, color, texture and flame rating
First article, sampling, appearance and fit check
Critical dimensions should be specified on drawing

steps.title: Confirm requirements and material
steps.body: Confirm use case, quantity, resin grade, cosmetic face and assembly needs.

steps.title: DFM and tool design
steps.body: Review shrinkage, draft, parting line, gate and cooling.

steps.title: Tool trial and correction
steps.body: Use T0/T1 samples to confirm dimensions and appearance, then correct if needed.

steps.title: Production
steps.body: Start volume production, sampling and packaging after parameters stabilize.

asideCta.title: Submit Requirements
asideCta.body: Upload drawings and project goals so engineers can review the service route.

related.title: Related Service Combinations
related.desc: Complex projects often combine multiple services. Review by geometry, volume, material and delivery goals.

items.title: CNC Machining Service
items.body: Milling, turning, mill-turn and hole-pattern machining for metal and engineering plastic parts.

items.title: Custom Manufacturing Service
items.body: Coordinates CNC, sheet metal, finishing, purchased parts and assembly for complex projects.

items.title: Metal Stamping Service
items.body: Single-stage or progressive stamping for brackets, springs, housings and connectors.

faq.title: Molding Service FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is Molding Service suitable for?
items.a: Molding Service is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a Molding Service quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Molding Service?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
